title: Disabling Strict-Transport-Security policy is security-sensitive
description: Disabling Strict-Transport-Security policy is security-sensitiveAnalyze your codeintentionality-completesecuritySecurity Hotspotcweexpress.jsWhen implementing the HTTPS protocol, the website mostly continue to support the HTTP protocol to redirect users to HTTPS when they request a HTTP version of the website. These redirects are not encrypted and are therefore vulnerable to man in the middle attacks. TheStrict-Transport-Security policy header(HSTS) set by an application instructs the web browser to convert any HTTP request to HTTPS.Web browsers that see the Strict-Transport-Security policy header for the first time record information specified in the header:themax-agedirective which specify how long the policy should be kept on the web browser.theincludeSubDomainsoptional directive which
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: HSTS max-age should be at least 31536000 (1 year)
